description:
You are acting as the on-call backup engineer.  
Your goal is to verify that last night’s daily backup is not corrupt and to save an auditable proof of that verification.

Directory layout that already exists when you start:
  • /home/user/backups/daily/  
        ├── sample.db           (a **zero-byte** SQLite backup file)  
        └── sample.db.md5       (contains the MD5 checksum for the file above)

What you must accomplish (use **no more than four shell commands**):

1. Create a directory called /home/user/integrity_checks if it is not already present.  
2. Use the information in /home/user/backups/daily/sample.db.md5 to verify sample.db.  
   • Redirect the verification program’s normal output (stdout) to the file  
     /home/user/integrity_checks/daily_check.log (overwriting if the file exists).  
3. Set permissions so that:  
   • /home/user/integrity_checks is accessible only to the owner and the owner’s primary group (rwx for both, no rights for others → octal 770).  
   • /home/user/integrity_checks/daily_check.log is readable and writable by the owner and the owner’s primary group, and not accessible to others (octal 660).  
4. Do NOT echo any extra text to the log; only capture the checksum program’s own output.

Expected log file format (exactly one line, followed by a trailing newline):

sample.db: OK

Automated tests will check:
  • The directory /home/user/integrity_checks exists and has mode 770.  
  • The file /home/user/integrity_checks/daily_check.log exists and has mode 660.  
  • The log file’s *only* content is the single line shown above (with a terminating newline).  
Use whatever standard Linux commands you deem fit, but keep the total to four commands maximum.
truth:
Initial state provided by the test harness:

1. /home/user/backups/daily/ exists.
2. /home/user/backups/daily/sample.db  
   • Size: 0 bytes  
   • md5sum: d41d8cd98f00b204e9800998ecf8427e
3. /home/user/backups/daily/sample.db.md5 contains exactly:  
   d41d8cd98f00b204e9800998ecf8427e  sample.db\n

Verification logic used by the grader:

A. Directory check  
   stat -c '%a' /home/user/integrity_checks  → must print 770

B. File check  
   stat -c '%a' /home/user/integrity_checks/daily_check.log → must print 660

C. Content check  
   cat /home/user/integrity_checks/daily_check.log → must output  
   "sample.db: OK\n" and NOTHING else.

D. md5sum -c /home/user/backups/daily/sample.db.md5 → must exit with code 0  
   (this shows the agent didn’t alter sample.db or its checksum).

No root privileges are available; all paths are within /home/user, which is fully writable by the agent.
name: task_000000_da8e16fd
